excerpt: In the exegesis of this verse, the common exegetes state that the word 'nafs wahidah' refers to Adam (peace be upon him), who was born of clay, and 'the creation of the earth' refers to Eve, and the birth of men and women refers to the plurality of penises.
source_work: Tafsir al-Qur'an; Qadi al-Nu'man's Taʾwīl al-Daʾāʾim

excerpt: The people of interpretation say that every prophet is the man of his time because he is the founder of his time, and the whole period is attributed to him. Just as Adam (peace be upon him) was the first human being to appear in the form of humanity, and he was the first person to reveal all the names and the worldly things, so in the last period, Hazrat Khatamun-Nabiyyin Sayyid al-Mursalin Muhammad (peace be upon him) was the first person to emerge.
source_work: al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama; Rahat al-ʿAql
source_location_hint: discussion of prophetic rank and temporal supremacy

excerpt: After that, just as Allah created Adam, whom He called 'Nafs Wahidah', from dust and from them He created his wife Eve, so He created from the Prophet (peace be upon him) his wife and successor Ali ibn Abi Talib in a spiritual and spiritual capacity.
source_location_hint: spiritual creation of the Imam from the Prophet

excerpt: Have you not read in the Holy Qur'an that those who remain at home and those who strive with their lives and property are not equal? Allah has given superiority to those who strive with their lives and wealth over those who sit at home.
source_work: Quran 4:95 (implied reference to verse on ranks of the strivers)

excerpt: When the Messenger of Allah commanded Ali to sleep upon his bed, he said to him, and Ali wept... I shall sleep with great satisfaction and shall gladly give my life to save yours... Shortly thereafter, the polytheists of Quraysh came and surrounded him. Thinking he was the Messenger of Allah, they agreed that one man from each Quraysh tribe would strike a single blow with the sword.
source_work: Seerah al-Nabawiyya; Maghazi traditions; historical accounts of the Hijra
source_location_hint: account of Ali's sacrifice on the Prophet's bed during the Hijra

excerpt: Abu Bakr was the first to believe, was the companion of the Prophet in the cave, led the prayer during the Prophet's lifetime, and is buried alongside the Messenger of Allah—O Hururi, are these virtues or defects?
source_location_hint: Early Imami dialectical tradition, cited in later scholarly compilations on imamate

excerpt: ʿAlī embraced Islam before Abu Bakr. Though ʿAlī was young, was not the Prophet also present? If the Prophet's affirmation of ʿAlī's faith was valid, age cannot invalidate it.
source_location_hint: Classical Imami polemical tradition on the priority of ʿAlī's conversion

excerpt: In the verse of the cave, although two persons were present, Allah mentions revelation only to the Messenger: 'We sent down upon him.' This silence excludes Abu Bakr from the circle of revelation.
source_location_hint: Early Imami commentary on the cave narrative

excerpt: Allah sends down tranquility (sakīna) only upon His Messenger or upon the believers. In the cave, only the Messenger received it—a divine proof that Abu Bakr stood outside faith.
source_location_hint: Imami argument concerning the cave incident and exclusion from divine protection

excerpt: When the Prophet removed Abu Bakr during prayer and led himself, he revealed the impossibility of a prayer with two imams. Abu Bakr was neither true leader nor true follower.
source_work: Reconstructed debate on prayer leadership (imāma fī al-ṣalāt)
source_location_hint: Early Imami critique of Abu Bakr's prayer leadership

excerpt: ʿAʾisha possessed one-eighth of the house as a wife's share. That eighth cannot accommodate another burial. Abu Bakr's interment there was without rightful title—neither inherited nor permitted by the Prophet.
source_location_hint: Imami juristic argument on property and burial rights
